info.name: Order Service — Integration Tests (US-1.4 & US-2.4)
info.description:
Postman collection mirroring the US-1.4 and US-2.4 Jest integration tests.

Covers:
  1. Health check (GET /health)
  2. Happy-path order placement (POST /orders → 201)
  3. Response contract validation (orderId UUID, status, correlationId, X-Correlation-Id header)
  4. EventBridge dispatch (indirect 201 assertion)
  5. Validation errors — 400 Bad Request
     a. Missing userId
     b. Invalid country code
     c. Negative totalAmount
     d. Empty items array
  6. Idempotency — duplicate orderId
  7. Unknown route — 404
  8. Notification Service Flow (US-2.4) — triggers SQS/SES flow using simulator email

Environment variables (configure in Postman Environment):
  BASE_URL          — e.g. https://<id>.execute-api.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com
  DEPLOY_ENV        — e.g. dev (used only in description; resource names derived from it)

Collection variables (auto-managed by pre-request / test scripts):
  orderId           — UUID generated per test run
  correlationId     — UUID generated per test run
  createdOrderId    — orderId captured from the happy-path request (used by idempotency test)

Run order:
  Use Postman Collection Runner or Newman in the order listed.
  Each folder is self-contained; happy-path folder MUST run before idempotency folder.

variable.description: HTTP API Gateway execute-api URL. Override in your Postman Environment.
Deployment environment — used in descriptions only.
Auto-generated UUID v4 per test. Set by pre-request scripts.
Auto-generated UUID v4 per test. Set by pre-request scripts.
orderId captured from the happy-path response. Used by the idempotency test.

item.name: 1 — Health Check
item.description:
Verifies the GET /health endpoint returns 200 {status:ok, service:order-service}.
Used by Route 53 health checks — must require no auth.

request.description:
Lightweight health check endpoint.
Expected: 200 {status: 'ok', service: 'order-service'}

item.name: 2 — Happy Path (POST /orders → 201)
item.description:
Places a valid order and validates the 201 response shape.
Generates a fresh UUID orderId and correlationId on each run.
Captures orderId into collection variable `createdOrderId` for use by the idempotency test.

exec: // Generate a fresh UUID v4 for orderId and correlationId
pm.test('Response body has orderId matching UUID v4 format', function () {
pm.test('Response header X-Correlation-Id is present', function () {
pm.test('Response header X-Correlation-Id matches request header', function () {
pm.test('correlationId in body matches X-Correlation-Id header', function () {

header.description: Custom correlation ID propagated to all downstream log entries and the response.

request.description:
Places a valid order. The handler:
  1. Validates the Zod schema → passes
  2. PutItem to DynamoDB (status=PLACED)
  3. Publishes to SNS (MESSAGING_MODE=SNS)
  4. PutEvents to EventBridge
  5. Returns 201 {orderId, status, correlationId}

item.name: POST /orders — orderId auto-generated (no orderId in body)

exec: pm.test('Server auto-generated a UUID v4 orderId', function () {

request.description: No orderId in the body — server must auto-generate a UUID v4.

item.name: 3 — EventBridge Dispatch (Indirect)
item.description:
The Order Lambda only returns 201 AFTER EB PutEvents succeeds.
A 201 response is proof the OrderPlaced event was dispatched to order-events-bus.

Note: For a direct SQS poll assertion, use the Jest integration test with EB_CATCHALL_QUEUE_URL.

exec: // The handler returns 502 if EB PutEvents throws.
// A 201 is therefore indirect proof that EB PutEvents succeeded.
pm.test('Status 201 — proves EB PutEvents was called successfully', function () {
pm.test('Response has orderId (EB event detail matches returned orderId)', function () {

item.name: 4 — Validation Errors (400 Bad Request)
item.description:
Tests every Zod schema validation rule.
All requests must return 400 with error=ValidationError.
No DynamoDB writes or SNS/EB publishes should occur.

exec: pm.test('details array is present (Zod issues)', function () {
pm.test('correlationId is present in error response', function () {

request.description: userId field is absent — must return 400 with Zod validation details.
Country must be exactly 2 uppercase chars (ISO 3166-1 alpha-2). "INDIA" is invalid.
Country must be UPPERCASE. "in" is invalid.
totalAmount must be strictly positive (> 0). -50 is invalid.
items array must have at least 1 element. Empty array is invalid.
currency must be exactly 3 uppercase chars (ISO 4217). "dollars" is invalid.

exec: pm.test('error is ValidationError (JSON parse failure)', function () {

request.description: Malformed JSON body — handler should catch JSON.parse error and return 400.

item.name: 5 — Idempotency (Duplicate orderId)
item.description:
Sends the same orderId twice.
Both requests must return 201 (ConditionalCheckFailedException is handled gracefully).
The second request must NOT throw or return 500.

Prerequisite: Folder 2 must have run first so that `createdOrderId` is set.

exec: // Generate a fixed orderId for resuse in the duplicate request below
pm.test('First request: orderId matches sent value', function () {
pm.test('Duplicate request: status is still 201 (idempotent)', function () {
pm.test('Duplicate request: same orderId returned', function () {
pm.test('Duplicate request: no 500 error (ConditionalCheckFailedException handled)', function () {

request.description:
Exact same payload as the previous request (same orderId).
Handler must return 201 via idempotent path, NOT throw 500.

item.name: 6 — Unknown Routes (404)
item.description: Verifies the handler returns 404 for unregistered routes.

request.description: DELETE /orders is not registered — must return 404.

item.name: 7 — Notification Service Flow (US-2.4)
item.description:
Integration test for the Notification Service (US-2.4).
Places an order for an Indian user to trigger the notification flow (SQS -> Lambda -> SES/DynamoDB).

exec: pm.test('Instruction: Check DynamoDB/SES manually based on response orderId', function () {
console.log('Notification flow triggered. Please query the Notifications DynamoDB table manually to ensure a record with status=SENT was created for orderId: ' + pm.response.json().orderId);

request.description: Places a valid order which gets fanned out via SNS to SQS, triggering Notification Lambda. It uses success@simulator.amazonses.com to ensure SES sends without bounces.

item.name: 8 — Helpdesk Service Flow (US-4.3)
item.description:
Integration test for the Helpdesk Service (US-4.3).
Places an order for a non-Indian user to trigger the Helpdesk Lambda via EventBridge.

exec: pm.test('Instruction: Check CloudWatch Logs manually for Helpdesk Lambda', function () {
console.log('Helpdesk flow triggered for US. Please query the Helpdesk Lambda logs to ensure it was invoked for orderId: ' + pm.response.json().orderId);

request.description: Places a valid order for a non-India user (US) which gets put to EventBridge, triggering Helpdesk Lambda.

exec: // Collection-level pre-request: ensure BASE_URL has no trailing slash
// Collection-level assertion: all responses must have X-Correlation-Id header
// (except non-JSON responses like 404 from API GW itself, which we skip)
pm.test('[Global] X-Correlation-Id header is present on all responses', function () {
